title: Bridge loop: bidirectional forwarding with loop detection
description:
## What
Implement the bridge event loop in bridge-matrix/src/bridge.rs: nie→Matrix forwarding and Matrix→nie forwarding with loop detection.

## Algorithm
1. struct Bridge { config: BridgeConfig, matrix: MatrixClient, sent_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> } — sent_ids tracks nie message_ids we sent (to avoid echo).
2. fn format_for_matrix(from_pub_id: &str, text: &str, prefix: Option<&str>) -> String: '[{prefix}{short_id}] {text}' where short_id = from_pub_id[..8].
3. fn format_for_nie(matrix_sender: &str, text: &str) -> ClearMessage: Chat { text: '[Matrix:{sender}] {text}' } — strips @localpart:domain to just localpart.
4. fn is_own_message(msg_id: &str, sent_ids: &HashSet) -> bool: returns sent_ids.contains(msg_id).
5. Bridge::run() connects to nie relay using nie_core::transport, starts axum HTTP server on config.listen_port (default 9000) for AS events, then runs select! loop: on nie ClearMessage::Chat from relay → forward to Matrix; on Matrix event from HTTP server → forward to nie relay.
6. HTTP server: POST /_matrix/app/v1/transactions/{txn_id} → deserialize AsTransaction, iterate events, for each text message: send to nie via BROADCAST.
7. Auth check on incoming: verify 'Authorization: Bearer {hs_token}' header before processing.

## Defensive programming
- never forward FileHeader/FileChunk or Payment messages to Matrix
- sent_ids is bounded: limit to last 1000 entries (VecDeque with capacity 1000, HashSet for O(1) lookup)
- always acknowledge AS transactions with {} even on error (or homeserver retries forever)
- never log message content (only metadata: sender pub_id prefix, room_id)

## Tests
- format_for_matrix_uses_prefix: assert '[nie] ab12cd34 some text' format
- format_for_nie_strips_domain: assert '@alice:example.com' produces '[Matrix:alice] text'
- is_own_message_detects_sent: add id to sent_ids, assert returns true
- is_own_message_allows_others: different id, assert returns false
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-bridge-matrix passes; bridge logic tests pass
